one_line_summary: A robotics simulation and training system for teaching robots navigation using imitation learning with whisker sensors
audience: Robotics researchers, students, and developers interested in robot navigation, imitation learning, and simulation-to-real transfer
primary_workflows: Interactive robot control and data collection
Evaluating trained models on benchmark scenarios

components.name: Utility Launcher
components.purpose: Central hub that launches all main tools in separate processes

components.name: Main Simulator
components.purpose: Interactive robot navigation simulator with visual feedback, data collection, and model inference

components.name: MLP Trainer
components.purpose: GUI for training Multi-Layer Perceptron models from collected navigation logs using imitation learning

components.name: Benchmark Utility
components.purpose: Tool for generating benchmark maps and evaluating trained models on standardized scenarios

components.name: Simulation Core
components.purpose: Shared physics and robot simulation logic used by both interactive and headless simulators

screen_candidates.title: Utility Launcher
screen_candidates.purpose: Central launcher providing one-click access to all main tools

screen_candidates.title: Main Simulator
screen_candidates.purpose: Primary robot navigation simulator with visual feedback and controls
notable_widgets: Control mode radio buttons (heading_drive, xy_strafe, heading_strafe)
Input mode radio buttons (keyboard, gamepad, model)

screen_candidates.title: MLP Trainer
screen_candidates.purpose: GUI for training imitation learning models from collected logs
notable_widgets: Activation function radio buttons (ReLU, Tanh, Leaky ReLU)

screen_candidates.title: Benchmark Utility
screen_candidates.purpose: Tool for generating benchmark maps and evaluating trained models

screen_candidates.title: Simulator Preferences Panel
screen_candidates.purpose: Configuration panel for simulation parameters within the main simulator
screen_candidates.construct_hint: Opens from Preferences button in main simulator

data_flow_notes: Data flows from simulator data collection (JSONL logs) → MLP trainer (parses logs, trains models) → trained models (JSON files) → benchmark utility (evaluates models). The simulator collects whisker sensor data, heading to target, and expert control actions. MLP trainer processes this into normalized features, trains neural networks, and outputs deployable models. Benchmark utility loads trained models and tests them on standardized scenarios, reporting success rates and performance metrics.
